cards.title: Qutub Minar
cards.subtitle: The Tower of Victory
cards.description: Delhi's soaring 73-metre fluted sandstone minaret, begun by Qutb-ud-din Aibak in 1199 to mark the founding of the Delhi Sultanate.

cards.title: The Iron Pillar
cards.subtitle: The Rustless Wonder
cards.description: A 4th-century Gupta-era iron column in the Qutb complex, famous for resisting rust for over 1,600 years.

cards.title: Red Fort
cards.subtitle: Lal Qila
cards.description: Shah Jahan's vast red-sandstone fortress-palace in Delhi, completed in 1648; the Prime Minister addresses the nation from its ramparts each Independence Day.

cards.title: Humayun's Tomb
cards.subtitle: The First Garden-Tomb
cards.description: The mid-16th-century Mughal mausoleum in Delhi, the subcontinent's first great garden-tomb and a forerunner of the Taj Mahal.

cards.title: Jama Masjid
cards.subtitle: The Friday Mosque
cards.description: Shah Jahan's grand red-sandstone and marble mosque in Old Delhi, completed in 1656 and among the largest in India.

cards.title: India Gate
cards.subtitle: The War Memorial
cards.description: The 42-metre sandstone arch on Delhi's central vista, honouring the Indian soldiers who died in the First World War and the Third Anglo-Afghan War.

cards.title: Lotus Temple
cards.subtitle: The Bahai House of Worship
cards.description: Delhi's marble Bahai temple, opened in 1986, shaped like a half-open lotus of twenty-seven petals and open to all faiths.

cards.title: Purana Qila
cards.subtitle: The Old Fort
cards.description: Delhi's oldest fort, raised by Sher Shah Suri and Humayun on a site long linked with the legendary Indraprastha.

cards.title: Taj Mahal
cards.subtitle: A Teardrop in Marble
cards.description: The white-marble mausoleum at Agra built by Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal, completed around 1653 and the most famous monument in India.

cards.title: Agra Fort
cards.subtitle: The Red Fort of Agra
cards.description: The great red-sandstone Mughal fort at Agra, residence of emperors from Akbar onward, where Shah Jahan spent his last years gazing at the Taj.

cards.title: Fatehpur Sikri
cards.subtitle: Akbar's Abandoned Capital
cards.description: The red-sandstone city built by Akbar in the 1570s as his capital, crowned by the towering Buland Darwaza, soon abandoned for want of water.

cards.title: Itimad-ud-Daulah
cards.subtitle: The Baby Taj
cards.description: The exquisite Agra tomb built by Nur Jahan for her father, the first Mughal monument faced entirely in white marble with inlaid stone.

cards.title: Bara Imambara
cards.subtitle: The Maze of Lucknow
cards.description: The grand 1784 Lucknow complex built by Nawab Asaf-ud-Daula, famed for its vast pillarless hall and the Bhulbhulaiya labyrinth.

cards.title: Dhamek Stupa
cards.subtitle: Where the Wheel Turned
cards.description: The massive cylindrical stupa at Sarnath marking the spot where the Buddha gave his first sermon, setting the wheel of dharma in motion.

cards.title: The Lion Capital
cards.subtitle: Ashoka's Pillar at Sarnath
cards.description: The Mauryan sandstone capital of four addorsed lions raised by Ashoka at Sarnath, adopted as the State Emblem of India.

cards.title: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
cards.subtitle: The Golden Temple of Shiva
cards.description: The revered Shiva temple on the Ganga at Varanasi, its spire and dome plated in gold gifted by Maharaja Ranjit Singh.

cards.title: Dashashwamedh Ghat
cards.subtitle: The Steps of the Ganga
cards.description: The most famous riverfront ghat at Varanasi, where the grand evening Ganga Aarti is performed before crowds of pilgrims.

cards.title: Jhansi Fort
cards.subtitle: Rani Lakshmibai's Stronghold
cards.description: The hilltop fort in Bundelkhand defended by Rani Lakshmibai during the 1857 uprising against the British.

cards.title: Amber Fort
cards.subtitle: The Amber Palace
cards.description: The hilltop fort-palace near Jaipur begun by Raja Man Singh in 1592, blending Rajput and Mughal styles above Maota Lake.

cards.title: Hawa Mahal
cards.subtitle: The Palace of Winds
cards.description: Jaipur's pink-sandstone façade of 1799, a honeycomb of 953 latticed windows from which royal women watched the street unseen.

cards.title: City Palace, Jaipur
cards.subtitle: Seat of the Maharajas
cards.description: The sprawling palace complex at the heart of Jaipur, begun by Sawai Jai Singh II and still home to the city's royal family.

cards.title: Jantar Mantar
cards.subtitle: The Stone Observatory
cards.description: Sawai Jai Singh II's 18th-century Jaipur observatory of giant masonry instruments, including the world's largest stone sundial.

cards.title: Mehrangarh Fort
cards.subtitle: The Citadel of Jodhpur
cards.description: The mighty fort rising 120 metres above the blue city of Jodhpur, founded by Rao Jodha in 1459 and lined with carved palaces.

cards.title: Jaisalmer Fort
cards.subtitle: The Golden Fort
cards.description: The 'Sonar Quila' of yellow sandstone rising from the Thar Desert, founded in 1156 and one of the world's few still-inhabited living forts.

cards.title: Chittorgarh Fort
cards.subtitle: The Pride of Mewar
cards.description: The vast hill fort of Mewar, scene of legendary Rajput sieges and jauhar, crowned by the Vijay Stambha tower of victory.

cards.title: Kumbhalgarh Fort
cards.subtitle: The Great Wall of India
cards.description: The 15th-century Mewar fort whose perimeter wall runs some 36 kilometres, among the longest in the world; birthplace of Maharana Pratap.

cards.title: City Palace, Udaipur
cards.subtitle: The Palace on Lake Pichola
cards.description: The grand lakeside palace complex of Mewar at Udaipur, begun by Udai Singh II, overlooking the island Lake Palace.

cards.title: Dilwara Temples
cards.subtitle: Marble Lacework
cards.description: The Jain temples at Mount Abu, built between the 11th and 13th centuries, renowned for astonishingly intricate white-marble carving.

cards.title: Ranakpur Jain Temple
cards.subtitle: The Temple of 1,444 Pillars
cards.description: The 15th-century marble Jain temple in Rajasthan dedicated to Adinatha, supported by 1,444 uniquely carved pillars.

cards.title: Junagarh Fort
cards.subtitle: The Fort of Bikaner
cards.description: The fort at Bikaner raised by Raja Rai Singh in the 1590s, never conquered, holding opulent palaces within its walls.

cards.title: Khajuraho Temples
cards.subtitle: Poetry in Stone
cards.description: The Chandela temples of the 10th and 11th centuries in Madhya Pradesh, famed for their sensuous and intricate sculpture.

cards.title: Sanchi Stupa
cards.subtitle: The Great Stupa
cards.description: The hemispherical Buddhist stupa commissioned by Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE, ringed by elaborately carved gateways (toranas).

cards.title: Gwalior Fort
cards.subtitle: The Gibraltar of India
cards.description: The hilltop fortress dominating Gwalior, prized for centuries and adorned with the blue-tiled Man Mandir palace.

cards.title: Orchha Fort
cards.subtitle: Bundela Splendour
cards.description: The riverside fort-palace of the Bundela kings at Orchha, with the soaring Jahangir Mahal and royal cenotaphs along the Betwa.

cards.title: Jahaz Mahal
cards.subtitle: The Ship Palace
cards.description: The 'ship palace' of Mandu, seeming to float between two lakes, built for the court of Sultan Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji.

cards.title: Bhimbetka Rock Shelters
cards.subtitle: India's Oldest Canvas
cards.description: The rock shelters of Madhya Pradesh whose cave paintings span many thousands of years, among the earliest traces of human life in India.

cards.title: Bhojeshwar Temple
cards.subtitle: The Unfinished Shiva Temple
cards.description: The 11th-century temple at Bhojpur housing one of India's largest single-stone Shiva lingams, left unfinished by King Bhoja.

cards.title: Rani ki Vav
cards.subtitle: The Queen's Stepwell
cards.description: The 11th-century stepwell at Patan, built as a memorial by Queen Udayamati, descending in carved terraces of over 500 sculptures.

cards.title: Modhera Sun Temple
cards.subtitle: Temple of the Sun
cards.description: The 11th-century Solanki temple to Surya at Modhera, aligned so the rising sun lights the sanctum at the equinoxes.

cards.title: Somnath Temple
cards.subtitle: The Eternal Shrine
cards.description: The Shiva temple on the Gujarat coast, first of the twelve jyotirlingas, destroyed and rebuilt many times across the centuries.

cards.title: Dwarkadhish Temple
cards.subtitle: The Abode of Krishna
cards.description: The towering five-storey temple to Krishna at Dwarka, one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites, flying a great flag changed daily.

cards.title: Adalaj Stepwell
cards.subtitle: A Well of Carved Light
cards.description: The five-storey 1498 stepwell near Ahmedabad, blending Hindu and Islamic motifs in cool carved galleries below ground.

cards.title: Dholavira
cards.subtitle: A Harappan City
cards.description: The Harappan city in the Kutch desert, over 4,500 years old, with sophisticated water reservoirs and an ancient signboard.

cards.title: Statue of Unity
cards.subtitle: The World's Tallest Statue
cards.description: The 182-metre statue of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel on the Narmada, unveiled in 2018 as the tallest statue in the world.

cards.title: Sabarmati Ashram
cards.subtitle: Gandhi's Home
cards.description: Mahatma Gandhi's riverside ashram at Ahmedabad, from which he set out on the 1930 Salt March to Dandi.

cards.title: Ajanta Caves
cards.subtitle: The Painted Caves
cards.description: The rock-cut Buddhist caves of Maharashtra, carved from the 2nd century BCE, celebrated for their ancient murals of the Buddha's lives.

cards.title: Ellora Caves
cards.subtitle: The Kailasa Temple
cards.description: The cave complex near Aurangabad where the colossal Kailasa temple was carved top-down from a single rock, spanning Buddhist, Hindu and Jain faiths.

cards.title: Elephanta Caves
cards.subtitle: The Cave of Shiva
cards.description: The rock-cut Shiva caves on an island off Mumbai, dominated by the great three-headed Trimurti sculpture.

cards.title: Gateway of India
cards.subtitle: The Arch of Mumbai
cards.description: The basalt triumphal arch on Mumbai's waterfront, completed in 1924, through which the last British troops departed India in 1948.

cards.title: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
cards.subtitle: A Victorian Gothic Marvel
cards.description: Mumbai's grand 1888 railway terminus, an exuberant fusion of Victorian Gothic and Indian styles designed by F. W. Stevens.

cards.title: Bibi Ka Maqbara
cards.subtitle: The Taj of the Deccan
cards.description: The 17th-century Aurangabad mausoleum built for Aurangzeb's wife, modelled on the Taj Mahal at smaller scale.

cards.title: Raigad Fort
cards.subtitle: Shivaji's Capital
cards.description: The hill fort in the Sahyadris where Shivaji was crowned Chhatrapati in 1674, capital of the Maratha kingdom.

cards.title: Daulatabad Fort
cards.subtitle: The Hill of Devagiri
cards.description: The formidable conical hill fort near Aurangabad, once Muhammad bin Tughlaq's short-lived imperial capital.

cards.title: Hampi
cards.subtitle: The Ruins of Vijayanagara
cards.description: The vast ruined capital of the Vijayanagara Empire on the Tungabhadra, strewn with temples, bazaars and the great Virupaksha shrine.

cards.title: The Stone Chariot
cards.subtitle: Hampi's Vittala Temple
cards.description: The iconic stone chariot shrine in Hampi's Vittala Temple complex, an emblem of Karnataka whose wheels were once able to turn.

cards.title: Mysore Palace
cards.subtitle: The Wodeyar Seat
cards.description: The opulent Indo-Saracenic palace of the Wodeyar kings at Mysuru, dazzlingly illuminated during the Dasara festival.

cards.title: Gol Gumbaz
cards.subtitle: The Whispering Dome
cards.description: The 17th-century tomb of Sultan Mohammed Adil Shah at Bijapur, crowned by one of the world's largest domes and a famous whispering gallery.

cards.title: Hoysaleswara Temple
cards.subtitle: The Halebidu Masterpiece
cards.description: The 12th-century Hoysala twin Shiva temple at Halebidu, its soapstone walls covered in friezes of unmatched detail.

cards.title: Chennakeshava Temple
cards.subtitle: The Belur Jewel
cards.description: The 12th-century Hoysala temple to Vishnu at Belur, built by King Vishnuvardhana and renowned for its sculpted bracket figures.

cards.title: Gomateshwara
cards.subtitle: The Colossus of Shravanabelagola
cards.description: The 17-metre monolithic statue of the Jain saint Bahubali, carved around 981 CE and anointed in the grand Mahamastakabhisheka.

cards.title: Badami Cave Temples
cards.subtitle: Chalukyan Rock Art
cards.description: The 6th-century rock-cut cave temples of the early Chalukyas at Badami, hewn from red sandstone cliffs above Agastya Lake.

cards.title: Pattadakal
cards.subtitle: A Cradle of Temple Building
cards.description: The Chalukyan temple complex in Karnataka where northern and southern temple styles met, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

cards.title: Brihadeeswarar Temple
cards.subtitle: The Great Chola Temple
cards.description: Raja Raja Chola's towering granite Shiva temple at Thanjavur, completed in 1010, its vimana among the tallest in the world.

cards.title: Meenakshi Amman Temple
cards.subtitle: The Temple of Madurai
cards.description: The vast Madurai temple to Meenakshi and Sundareswarar, its towering gopurams covered in thousands of brightly painted figures.

cards.title: Shore Temple
cards.subtitle: The Temple by the Sea
cards.description: The early-8th-century Pallava temple at Mahabalipuram standing against the Bay of Bengal, one of the oldest stone temples in South India.

cards.title: Pancha Rathas
cards.subtitle: The Five Chariots
cards.description: The five monolithic Pallava shrines at Mahabalipuram, each carved as a 'ratha' from a single rock in the 7th century.

cards.title: Arjuna's Penance
cards.subtitle: The Descent of the Ganges
cards.description: The giant 7th-century Pallava bas-relief at Mahabalipuram, among the largest open-air rock reliefs in the world.

cards.title: Airavatesvara Temple
cards.subtitle: The Chariot of Darasuram
cards.description: The 12th-century Chola temple at Darasuram, shaped as a great stone chariot and famed for its musical stone steps.

cards.title: Ramanathaswamy Temple
cards.subtitle: The Corridor of Rameswaram
cards.description: The island temple at Rameswaram with the longest pillared corridor of any Hindu temple, both a Char Dham and a jyotirlinga site.

cards.title: Rockfort Temple
cards.subtitle: The Rock of Tiruchirappalli
cards.description: The temple complex perched atop an ancient rock outcrop at Tiruchirappalli, reached by hundreds of steps cut into the stone.

cards.title: Charminar
cards.subtitle: The Four Minarets
cards.description: Hyderabad's 1591 monument of four grand arches and minarets, raised by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah at the founding of the city.

cards.title: Golconda Fort
cards.subtitle: The Citadel of Diamonds
cards.description: The great granite fort of the Qutb Shahis near Hyderabad, once the centre of the world's diamond trade, with remarkable acoustics.

cards.title: Qutb Shahi Tombs
cards.subtitle: The Royal Necropolis
cards.description: The domed tombs of the Qutb Shahi sultans set in gardens near Golconda, blending Persian, Pathan and Hindu styles.

cards.title: Ramappa Temple
cards.subtitle: The Floating-Brick Temple
cards.description: The 13th-century Kakatiya Shiva temple in Telangana, built with bricks light enough to float; a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

cards.title: Warangal Fort
cards.subtitle: The Kakatiya Capital
cards.description: The ruined fort of the Kakatiya dynasty at Warangal, famed for its ornate carved gateways, the Kakatiya Kala Thoranam.

cards.title: Lepakshi Temple
cards.subtitle: The Hanging Pillar
cards.description: The 16th-century Vijayanagara temple in Andhra Pradesh, known for its hanging pillar, giant monolithic Nandi and ceiling murals.

cards.title: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
cards.subtitle: The Reclining Vishnu
cards.description: The Thiruvananthapuram temple to Vishnu reclining on the serpent Anantha, famed for its carved gopuram and legendary vaults.

cards.title: Mattancherry Palace
cards.subtitle: The Dutch Palace
cards.description: The Kochi palace built by the Portuguese and renovated by the Dutch, lined with some of India's finest Hindu murals.

cards.title: Bekal Fort
cards.subtitle: The Keyhole by the Sea
cards.description: The large keyhole-shaped laterite fort on the Kerala coast, its observation tower looking out over the Arabian Sea.

cards.title: Basilica of Bom Jesus
cards.subtitle: The Shrine of Old Goa
cards.description: The 16th-century baroque basilica in Old Goa holding the relics of St Francis Xavier; a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

cards.title: Sé Cathedral
cards.subtitle: The Great Church of Goa
cards.description: One of Asia's largest churches, raised by the Portuguese in Old Goa and home to the famed Golden Bell.

cards.title: Fort Aguada
cards.subtitle: The Watchtower of Goa
cards.description: The 17th-century Portuguese coastal fort and lighthouse guarding the mouth of the Mandovi against the Dutch and Marathas.

cards.title: Victoria Memorial
cards.subtitle: The Marble Hall of Kolkata
cards.description: The grand white-marble memorial to Queen Victoria in Kolkata, completed in 1921 and now a museum of the Raj era.

cards.title: Howrah Bridge
cards.subtitle: The Gateway to Kolkata
cards.description: The great cantilever bridge over the Hooghly, opened in 1943, carrying vast daily crowds without a single pier in the river.

cards.title: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
cards.subtitle: Ramakrishna's Shrine
cards.description: The 19th-century Kali temple on the Hooghly near Kolkata, founded by Rani Rashmoni and linked to the saint Ramakrishna.

cards.title: Konark Sun Temple
cards.subtitle: The Chariot of the Sun
cards.description: The 13th-century Odisha temple to Surya built as a colossal stone chariot with twelve pairs of carved wheels drawn by seven horses.

cards.title: Jagannath Temple
cards.subtitle: The Lord of the Universe
cards.description: The great 12th-century temple at Puri to Jagannath, whose deities ride out each year on towering chariots in the Rath Yatra.

cards.title: Lingaraja Temple
cards.subtitle: The Heart of Bhubaneswar
cards.description: The 11th-century Kalinga-style Shiva temple dominating Bhubaneswar, the ancient 'temple city' of Odisha.

cards.title: Udayagiri Caves
cards.subtitle: The Jain Hermitage
cards.description: The rock-cut caves near Bhubaneswar carved for Jain monks under King Kharavela, bearing the famous Hathigumpha inscription.

cards.title: Hazarduari Palace
cards.subtitle: The Palace of a Thousand Doors
cards.description: The 19th-century Italianate palace at Murshidabad, built for the Nawabs of Bengal, with its thousand doors, many of them false.

cards.title: Mahabodhi Temple
cards.subtitle: Where the Buddha Awoke
cards.description: The temple at Bodh Gaya beside the Bodhi tree under which the Buddha attained enlightenment, among Buddhism's holiest sites.

cards.title: Nalanda Mahavihara
cards.subtitle: The Ancient University
cards.description: The ruins of the great Buddhist monastic university in Bihar, a centre of learning from the 5th century until its destruction around 1200.

cards.title: Golghar
cards.subtitle: The Great Granary
cards.description: The beehive-shaped colonial granary built at Patna in 1786 after a famine, climbed by two sweeping spiral stairways.

cards.title: Golden Temple
cards.subtitle: Harmandir Sahib
cards.description: The holiest shrine of Sikhism at Amritsar, its gilded sanctum set in the sacred Amrit Sarovar pool and open on all four sides.

cards.title: Jallianwala Bagh
cards.subtitle: A Garden of Memory
cards.description: The walled Amritsar garden where troops fired on a peaceful gathering in 1919, a turning point in India's freedom struggle.

cards.title: Leh Palace
cards.subtitle: The Roof of Ladakh
cards.description: The nine-storey 17th-century royal palace overlooking Leh, built by King Sengge Namgyal in the manner of Tibet's Potala.

cards.title: Thiksey Monastery
cards.subtitle: The Little Potala
cards.description: The hilltop Buddhist gompa near Leh, resembling the Potala Palace, home to a giant statue of the future Buddha Maitreya.

cards.title: Key Monastery
cards.subtitle: The Fort of Spiti
cards.description: The thousand-year-old Tibetan Buddhist monastery perched on a crag high in the Spiti Valley of Himachal Pradesh.

cards.title: Kamakhya Temple
cards.subtitle: The Shakti Shrine of Assam
cards.description: The hilltop temple near Guwahati to the goddess Kamakhya, among the most revered Shakti Peethas and centre of the Ambubachi Mela.

cards.title: Rang Ghar
cards.subtitle: The Colosseum of the East
cards.description: The two-storey 18th-century Ahom pavilion near Sivasagar in Assam, one of Asia's oldest surviving amphitheatres.
